join_order.build: sub(columns: fy, cik, name — direct array, fully cached)
join_order.notes: num.adsh_code == sub row_id; no hash join needed — direct index into sub/fy.bin[adsh_code], sub/cik.bin[adsh_code], sub/name.bin[adsh_code]. Sub columns are ~860 KB total and fit in L3 cache.

filters.notes: Load usd_code at runtime from shared/uom.dict by matching string 'USD'. Never hardcode. ~34.3M rows pass.
IEEE 754 NaN sentinel for NULL. Applied together with uom filter.
Direct array read. Combined with uom+value filters yields ~18% of num rows (~7.1M). Applied after uom+value to avoid wasted lookups.

subquery.description: Both the HAVING subquery (GROUP BY cik) and the main query (GROUP BY name, cik) require identical scan+filter logic. Compute both in a single pass: maintain two thread-local hash maps simultaneously. After merging, derive threshold T = AVG over cik-level sums, then apply HAVING to the (name,cik)-level map.

data_structures.sub_columns_cache: mmap sub/fy.bin, sub/cik.bin, sub/name.bin into memory at startup — 86,135 rows each, ~860 KB total, fits in L3
data_structures.thread_local_cik_map: compact_hash_map(int32_t cik → double sum), pre-sized next_pow2(8886*2)=16384 slots — fits in L2 cache per thread
data_structures.thread_local_name_cik_map: compact_hash_map(int64_t composite_key → double sum) where key = (int64_t)name_code << 32 | (uint32_t)cik, pre-sized next_pow2(16000*2)=32768 slots
data_structures.threshold_scalar: double T computed as AVG of merged cik_map values after parallel merge
data_structures.output_buffer: std::vector of (name_code, cik, sum) filtered by HAVING; partial_sort or std::nth_element + sort for top-100

parallelism.thread_local_state: each thread owns independent cik_map and name_cik_map; no synchronization during scan
parallelism.merge_phase: after all morsels complete, reduce thread-local maps sequentially (maps are small, merge is O(8886) per thread); compute T; apply HAVING; decode; sort top-100

indexes.notes: Read indexes/num_zonemaps.bin. For each 100K-row block, skip if uom_min > usd_code OR uom_max < usd_code. Since num is sorted by (uom, ddate) and usd_code=0 is the lowest non-null code, USD rows cluster contiguously at the start. Zone maps allow skipping trailing non-USD blocks, reducing IO on HDD.

output.decode_step: after HAVING filter, decode name_code via sub/name.dict for surviving groups only (few hundred at most)
